ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Amu na Unwoti úye Kain ɔnini, “Bi ɔsu na ábla likpidi bonwu lɛmɛni udu? Nu! Mɔɔnu ƒ'ɔwani ubugya ɔni nwi kalɔ. Use aani ukyuli mɔɔkpali ɔnii nkpo nte-nwu.
translated: Amu said to Unwoti, "Is it not true that Kain is a man? Yes! But he does not have the heart of a man. He is like a woman who has given birth to only one child.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Na mbo ɔmantɛ bakɔ ku bebuo ala betine ku baƒonu ku bese ku kabla bablane bananabi ku belekubi. Na ɔƒaani ni, mina Ati, mɔɔsia-a kenye kamɛni kalɛ na mina lɛlɔ lɛda-a ebiti.’ ”
translated: And the people of the earth will see the righteousness, truth, peace, faith, love, and all the good fruits of the Spirit. And they will say, ‘Surely, the Lord our God is with these people, and we did not know it.’ ”

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Mmɔ na bana inyɔ ƒui anumi ébimi na bánu báti ka bakɛ kasɔngya. Anwuɔsu béde kugyii evete bonwu adi básala na béti bábɛla lɛlɔ kasɔngya.
translated: The chief and his children are going to the farm to harvest corn. The women are going to the river to fetch water and wash clothes.

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: Na Henok ɔ́siaku Katɔti kama geen ɔni na Katɔti útiku-nwu ɔ́tɔlɔku na ɔdi ɔ́sɔ útanu-nwu kudii.
translated: Enoch was going to Katoti but when he reached Katoti, he did not meet anyone and so he turned back.

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ka ɔ́su lɛni ni, bédie Rebeka ulu ku ulekubi ɔni mɔɔnu-nwu lɛlɔ ku Abraham kabla ɔblane ɔmmɔ ku bekyuli bani nsiaku-nwu.
translated: And it came to pass, before Rebekah had finished speaking, that behold, the servant of Abraham came out with his camels to the well.
